description: Single-session pipeline phase-gate catalog (Campaign 2026-07-07, SS2). SSoT for every interactive AskUserQuestion + END-TURN gate across the project/design/plan/build/deploy phase skills, with a per-gate non-interactive policy. Read by resolve_gate_policy.py; consumed by phase-runner subagents under run_config.mode == 'single_session'. The mechanism is INERT (every gate resolves to 'interactive') for any config that is not an explicit single_session run — a standalone or adopted project is unaffected. docs/gate-catalog.md is GENERATED from this file (gate_policy.render_catalog_markdown) — edit here, then regenerate.

policies.auto-default: Under single_session the subagent proceeds with the documented default_answer and does NOT call AskUserQuestion / end the turn.
policies.orchestrator-approve: Under single_session the subagent STOPS and returns a gate-pending result to the orchestrator to surface to a human; it never auto-answers. A future autonomous orchestrator MAY surface-and-wait or auto-approve ONLY gates with constitution=false; a constitution=true orchestrator-approve gate (e.g. skip-test-layer, >3 failed fixes, a missing secret) always reaches a human.
policies.hard-stop: Under single_session the subagent ALWAYS stops for an explicit human decision; no autonomy setting bypasses it. Constitution-locked class (PROD deploy, destructive SQL, migration-apply failure, rollback).

constitution_lock: INVARIANT for SS3+: a gate with constitution=true is NEVER auto-approvable by any autonomy level, regardless of whether its policy is orchestrator-approve or hard-stop. constitution=true is the real lock signal; policy only distinguishes 'surface to the orchestrator' (orchestrator-approve) from 'a human must decide here, full stop' (hard-stop).

pending_phases.note: test / changelog / security phase gates are a deliberate SS2 follow-up (not silently dropped). This catalog is additive — extend `gates` to cover them. The sub-iterate spec scoped SS2 to project/design/plan/build/deploy.

gates.summary: A.1 first-turn: full pipeline vs standalone spec. Skipped when shipwright_run_config.json already exists (the orchestrator pre-writes it).

gates.default_answer: The project name inferred from the seed description (or the current directory).
gates.summary: Inline/chat only: where to create the project. Skipped when the pipeline supplies the project path.
status==in_progress AND current_step!=project. A state anomaly; the orchestrator owns sequencing so it should not fire in a well-formed single-session run.
Existing session/state conflict at setup. 'Start fresh' discards existing work and must never be auto-picked; a fresh pipeline dir has no conflict.

gates.default_answer: Proceed with the inferred assumptions (derived from the seed requirements).
gates.summary: Pre-interview: surface inferred assumptions for correction (inline prose, borderline gate).

gates.default_answer: Answer each requirements question from the seed description/spec and record it as an assumption; a pipeline run always carries a seed to derive from.
gates.summary: The requirements interview (adaptive depth). The primary interactivity surface; the pipeline always supplies a seed.

gates.default_answer: Approve the proposed split/decomposition manifest as-is (agent-generated and regenerable).
gates.summary: Step 4: confirm the proposed split-structure manifest before creating split directories.
supabase-nextjs profile only: the external Supabase project ref. Cannot be invented; surface to a human or defer linking.
supabase-nextjs profile only: the Supabase access token (a secret). Never fabricate or hardcode (constitution NEVER); surface or defer linking.
status==in_progress AND current_step!=design. State anomaly; should not fire in a well-formed single-session run.

gates.default_answer: Use the site-extracted brand tokens as the foundation (they remain overridable downstream).
gates.summary: Step 2.5: confirm auto-extracted brand tokens. Fires only when an existing website is known.

gates.default_answer: Untitled UI flavor + Clean & Modern character + Sidebar nav (or the Step 2.5 extracted tokens if present); the visual direction is re-checked at design.preview-approval.
gates.summary: Step 3: 3-5 design-system/brand/layout questions. Every field has a documented default.

gates.default_answer: Accept the FR-derived screen list (re-verified by the FR-Coverage Gate at finalize).
gates.summary: Confirm the proposed screen list after the design interview.
Step 3.5: first pixels-seen checkpoint (3 preview screens). SENSITIVE (Sven): a human must eyeball the visual direction — never auto-approved.

gates.default_answer: Accept the screen-list-derived navigation (mechanical; cheaply corrected later via Chrome Change Propagation).
gates.summary: Step 3.7: confirm the derived chrome/navigation structure.
Step 8.5: all-screens sign-off + phase exit. SENSITIVE (Sven): the design phase's human approval gate — subagent runs the automated FR-Coverage self-check, then stops for a human.
Single-screen iteration mode only (@file HTML arg). Not reached in a fresh from-scratch pipeline run.
Upload-integration mode only (--upload). Mapping uploaded artifacts to screens is human intent; not reached in a fresh pipeline run.
No/invalid spec path at startup. The orchestrator supplies the spec, so this should not fire; on failure return control rather than hang.
status==in_progress AND current_step!=plan. State anomaly; the orchestrator owns sequencing.

gates.default_answer: Answer each design-decision question from the loaded project context (spec, CLAUDE.md, conventions.md, architecture.md) and record it as an assumption in the plan interview record. Human plan-review is deferred to SS3's cross-phase gate-list (Sven, 2026-07-07).
gates.summary: The design-decision interview (adaptive 3-5 questions). Largest interactivity surface; answered from context under single_session.

gates.default_answer: Self-summarize and proceed to write the plan without blocking on outline approval (a context-hygiene optimization; downstream review/verification still runs).
gates.summary: Fires only on self-assessed large context: 5-10 bullet outline for approval before writing the plan.

gates.default_answer: Option 2: skip external review, fall back to the mandatory self-review ('2x denken') pass, and log the opt-out reason ('non-interactive single-session run; no OPENROUTER key provisioned').
gates.summary: Step 5 Branch B: no external-review API key. Not silently skippable; the self-review fallback preserves the gate.

gates.default_answer: Autonomously integrate high-confidence findings and decline the rest with a logged reason; every finding is recorded to decision_log.md (audit trail preserved).
gates.summary: Step 5 Branch A: adjudicate external-review findings. Soft gate ('discuss') — auto-integrate + log under single_session.
No/invalid section-file arg. The orchestrator passes the path in the brief; a miss is a wiring bug — return control.
status==in_progress AND current_step!=build. State anomaly; the orchestrator owns sequencing.
validate_env.py reports missing required env vars. Secret/env provisioning is a human/orchestrator responsibility; never auto-skip.
A generated migration contains DROP/TRUNCATE/DELETE-without-WHERE. Constitution ASK-FIRST 'regardless of autonomy level' — irreversible data loss.
Migration preflight (CLI/auth/connectivity) failed before apply. Environment repair the subagent can't self-do; return control (do not run tests against a stale schema).
Migration apply failed mid-run (DB may be in partial state). Constitution ASK-FIRST 'stop immediately' — human diagnosis required.

gates.default_answer: Log the manual step as a warning, skip tests in blocks_tests_for, and flag it in the result JSON (the existing autonomous-mode degrade).
gates.summary: A post-apply manual step matches the applied migration. Build already degrades this in autonomous mode.
Frontend changed but no dev-server/URL resolves for browser verify. Do NOT silently skip; return control for a dev_url/port.
browser_verify still failing after 3 browser-fixer retries. Return the diagnosis to the orchestrator; don't auto-swallow a reproduced UI failure.
2 failed fixes same root cause, or 3 total. Constitution ASK-FIRST + NEVER 'loop >3 debugging attempts without escalating' — surface attempt-log + alternatives.

gates.default_answer: Auto-accept and fix every finding, logging each as 'auto-fixed (autonomous mode)' — mirrors build's existing autonomous behaviour.
gates.summary: Step 6b guided-mode per-finding triage. Already fully suppressed (auto-accept) in build's autonomous mode.
status==in_progress AND current_step!=deploy. State anomaly; the orchestrator owns sequencing.
validate_env.py reports missing required deploy vars. Provisioning deploy secrets is a human responsibility; auto-skip risks a broken deploy.
Tests missing/red at the deploy gate. Constitution ASK-FIRST 'skipping test layers'; in a healthy pipeline the prior test phase passed, so this should not fire.
Migrations exist but a prerequisite (config.toml / project link / access token) is missing. Credential/linking provisioning is outside the subagent's reach.

gates.default_answer: Apply the pending DEV migrations when supports_idempotent_apply is true (non-prod, preflight ok) — constitution-permitted; otherwise surface to the orchestrator.
gates.summary: Pending DEV migrations. Constitution explicitly permits non-prod idempotent apply; the non-idempotent case escalates.
PROD + migrations: present dry-run, require explicit confirmation before apply. Constitution ASK-FIRST 'PROD deployments' + 'migration apply'.
A destructive migration was flagged (any target, DEV or PROD). Constitution ASK-FIRST 'destructive SQL — always confirm, even in autonomous mode'.
Post-apply migration_verifier failed (live schema mismatch). Couples rollback + migration-failure ASK-FIRST classes; 'Override and continue' must never be auto-picked.
A post-apply manual step matches a just-applied migration. An out-of-band human action; the subagent cannot perform or truthfully confirm it.
The canonical PROD deploy confirmation (after the backup clone). Constitution ASK-FIRST 'PROD deployments — always confirm + backup, regardless of autonomy level'. The strongest gate.
Operator-initiated PROD rollback (--rollback flag only). Constitution ASK-FIRST 'Rollback decisions'; not part of the normal deploy flow.

gates.default_answer: Auto-revert to last-known-good and ANNOUNCE it (rollback-discipline Pattern 3: a silent rollback is worse than the failure). Not an AskUserQuestion gate today — the documented auto exception to the rollback ASK-FIRST rule.
gates.summary: Smoke test failed → automatic rollback (git-tag revert for DEV, clone restore for PROD). A documented non-gate; announces itself.
